description: Stoic philosophy cartridge — ten core principles for resilience, clarity, and sovereign decision-making.
ethical_guardrail: Never weaponize discomfort as justification for neglecting self-care or the well-being of others.

rules.name: Dichotomy of Control
rules.principle: Distinguish what is within your control (your choices, judgments, actions) from what is not (others' opinions, external events).
rules.insight_template: Focus on what you can control in this situation: your response to {situation}. Release attachment to outcomes beyond your influence.

rules.name: Memento Mori
rules.principle: Reflect on mortality to clarify priorities and eliminate trivial concerns.
rules.insight_template: Time is finite. Ask: if this were your last day, would {situation} still matter? Let mortality sharpen your focus.

rules.name: Amor Fati
rules.principle: Love your fate — embrace everything that happens, including hardship, as necessary and beneficial.
rules.insight_template: Rather than resisting {situation}, ask: what strength or insight can only be forged through this exact difficulty?

rules.name: Voluntary Discomfort
rules.principle: Deliberately practice hardship to build resilience and reduce fear of loss.
rules.insight_template: Growth lives outside comfort. Consider deliberately facing {situation} to strengthen your capacity for adversity.

rules.name: Premeditatio Malorum
rules.principle: Pre-meditate on potential adversities so you are never caught off-guard.
rules.insight_template: Visualize the worst outcome of {situation}. Once you have faced it mentally, you are prepared — and the fear loses its grip.

rules.name: Present Moment Awareness
rules.principle: The present is all you possess. Do not squander it reliving the past or dreading the future.
rules.insight_template: Your attention is on {situation}, but only this moment is real. Return to what is actionable right now.

rules.name: Virtue as the Sole Good
rules.principle: External goods (wealth, fame, pleasure) are preferred indifferents. Only virtue — wisdom, courage, justice, temperance — is truly good.
rules.insight_template: The pursuit of {situation} may be a preferred indifferent. Ask: does this path cultivate wisdom, courage, justice, or temperance?

rules.name: Sympatheia (Interconnectedness)
rules.principle: All things are woven together. Your actions ripple through a connected whole.
rules.insight_template: You are part of a larger whole. Consider how your response to {situation} affects the web of relationships around you.

rules.name: The Inner Citadel
rules.principle: Your mind is an impregnable fortress. No external event can disturb you without your consent.
rules.insight_template: External events around {situation} have no power over your inner state unless you grant it. Guard your ruling faculty.

rules.name: The View from Above
rules.principle: Zoom out to a cosmic perspective. Most troubles shrink to insignificance when viewed from sufficient distance.
rules.insight_template: Step back from {situation}. Viewed from above — across years, continents, lifetimes — how significant is this truly?
